summary: Library auto-initializes Sentry with hardcoded author DSN on import, exfiltrating consumer error and profiling data
details: On import, the package's main entry re-exports dist/common/instrument.js, whose top-level code unconditionally calls Sentry.init with a hardcoded DSN pointing at the author's Sentry project (o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920), with tracesSampleRate and profilesSampleRate both set to 1.0. Any application that requires or imports finup-mongo-library will install global Sentry error, exception, and profiling handlers that ship the consumer application's error payloads (stack traces, captured request bodies, profiling samples) to a Sentry organization controlled by the package author, without the consumer opting in. Because exception payloads commonly include request data and surrounding context, this can leak PII and internal application state from the consumer's runtime to a third party. This is a silent-relay pattern: the advertised purpose (a MongoDB helper library) does not require any telemetry, and routing of the consumer's data to the author's destination is hard-coded and unconditional.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
